explanation: The requirement states: 'At the end of an inspiration phase, if the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI, an Inspiratory Pause shall start.' This means, whenever (i) inspiratoryPhaseEnd occurs (i.e., end of inspiration), and (ii) inspiratoryPauseButton is set (i.e., user via the GUI requested Inspiratory Pause), then (iii) inspiratoryPause must start (become true, i.e., be set). This is a classic situation-action (whenever X & Y, then Z), best captured by a response LTL pattern. I represent the antecedent by (inspiratoryPhaseEnd & inspiratoryPauseButton), and the consequent by inspiratoryPause. The global response template (index 40) G((A & B) -> C) fits this: globally, whenever both inspiratoryPhaseEnd and inspiratoryPauseButton occur at the same time, we require inspiratoryPause to be triggered immediately (in the same step). This captures the requirement as a safety property: at all times, if at the end of an inspiration the pause was set by GUI, the pause will start.

The natural language states that at the end of an inspiration phase, if the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI, then an Inspiratory Pause should start. This is an obligation that relates the triggering condition (end of inspiration AND GUI button set) to the expected effect (Inspiratory Pause starts). The LTL property expresses that globally, whenever the end of the inspiratory phase occurs AND the inspiratoryPauseButton is set, then in the next state, inspiratoryPause becomes true. This matches a classic response pattern to an event with a guard condition. The appropriate template here is the one encoding 'G((A & B) -> (X C))'. Here, bool_exp1 = inspiratoryPhaseEnd, bool_exp2 = inspiratoryPauseButton, bool_exp3 = inspiratoryPause. N_DURATION is not needed.

The requirement states that at the end of inspiration, if the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI (represented by 'inspiratoryPauseButton'), the Inspiratory Pause should begin ('inspiratoryPause'). This is a classic response relationship: whenever 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' happens and at the same moment 'inspiratoryPauseButton' is true, then 'inspiratoryPause' should follow (start). Template 41 closely matches this causality and allows us to express the conjunction condition ('at insp. phase end AND GUI pause set' triggers pause signal). Here, N_DURATION is set (e.g., 1) to guarantee immediate action upon condition.

The requirement states that at the end of an inspiration phase, if the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI, an Inspiratory Pause must start. In the given atomic propositions, 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' marks the end of the inspiration phase, and 'inspiratoryPauseButton' indicates that the Inspiratory Pause has been set by the GUI. The effect that must happen if these two are true is that 'inspiratoryPause' (meaning an Inspiratory Pause is active) must start. This LTL property makes sure that whenever both the end of the inspiration phase and the GUI has set the Inspiratory Pause (i.e., inspiratoryPhaseEnd & inspiratoryPauseButton) are true, it is immediately followed by inspiratoryPause. The 'X' temporal operator means 'in the next state,' ensuring the pause starts right after the transition. This matches the original requirement in both timing and causality.

The requirement states: at the end of the inspiratory phase, if the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI, an Inspiratory Pause shall start. This can be expressed using a trigger-response pattern. Specifically, whenever both 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' is true and 'inspiratoryPauseButton' is true (the GUI button to set an inspiratory pause is pressed), then 'inspiratoryPause' should be made true in the next step. Template 41 is suited for this, using bool_exp2 = inspiratoryPhaseEnd & inspiratoryPauseButton, and bool_exp3 = inspiratoryPause. The unused bool_exp1 and bool_exp4 are set to valid vacuous boolean expressions ('true').

The property describes that: Whenever the inspiratory phase ends ('inspiratoryPhaseEnd') AND the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI ('inspiratoryPauseButton'), then an Inspiratory Pause ('inspiratoryPause') should start. We capture this as a safety/liveness property: globally, if both 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' and 'inspiratoryPauseButton' are true, then 'inspiratoryPause' must immediately be true. Template 41 allows us to express this with a globally-scoped, conjunctive antecedent, and an atomic proposition as the consequent.

This fits the requirement as it uses the meaning of each involved atomic proposition (end of inspiration phase, Inspiratory Pause requested, Inspiratory Pause actually happens), and directly ties the temporal condition in the requirement (immediate start at the end of phase) to the LTL structure.

The requirement states: At the end of the inspiratory phase, if the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI, then an Inspiratory Pause shall start. This means that whenever the end of the inspiratory phase occurs (signaled by 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd'), and also the Inspiratory Pause button has been set by the GUI ('inspiratoryPauseButton' is true), then as a consequence, an Inspiratory Pause ('inspiratoryPause') should begin in the next step. The most fitting template is an implication that, globally, if both inspiratoryPhaseEnd and inspiratoryPauseButton are true, then in the next step inspiratoryPause will be true. Therefore, we use the template: G((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> (X bool_exp3)), with: bool_exp1 = inspiratoryPhaseEnd, bool_exp2 = inspiratoryPauseButton, and bool_exp3 = inspiratoryPause.

The requirement states that at the end of an inspiration phase, if the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI (represented by 'inspiratoryPauseButton'), an Inspiratory Pause (represented by 'inspiratoryPause') shall start. This describes a conditional reaction to an event. The template 'G (inspiratoryPhaseEnd & inspiratoryPauseButton -> X inspiratoryPause)' (ID 11) directly expresses this: whenever both 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd' and 'inspiratoryPauseButton' are true at any step (i.e., end of inspiration and Inspiratory Pause set), then in the next step, 'inspiratoryPause' must hold, i.e., the pause starts immediately after the inspiration phase ends if requested by the GUI. This models the intended behavior precisely as stated in the natural language requirement.

The natural language requirement specifies that at the end of the inspiration phase, if the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI (i.e., 'inspiratoryPauseButton' is true), an Inspiratory Pause ('inspiratoryPause') should start. Thus, this behavior is best captured by the LTL template: 'G (inspiratoryPhaseEnd & inspiratoryPauseButton -> X inspiratoryPause)'. This states that globally (at all times), whenever the end of inspiratory phase occurs and the pause button is set, in the next state an Inspiratory Pause shall be active.

This requirement specifies that at the end of an inspiration phase, if the Inspiratory Pause is set by the GUI, then an Inspiratory Pause shall start. The temporal relation is: whenever ('inspiratoryPhaseEnd' AND 'inspiratoryPauseButton') is true, 'inspiratoryPause' must be true in the next step. The template 'G((bool_exp2) -> (X bool_exp3))' is ideal for expressing this, where bool_exp2 = inspiratoryPhaseEnd & inspiratoryPauseButton and bool_exp3 = inspiratoryPause. This guarantees the correct reactive behavior across all steps.
